code: @Manual{,   title = {VMAorder: Tools for Vector Moving Average Order Estimation},   author = {Enxin Qin},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://qin01546-ops.github.io/VMAorder}, }

title: Tools for Vector Moving Average Order Estimation
text: VMAorder provides tools simulation, statistic construction, order estimation, stationarity checking, visualization vector moving-average (VMA) models. package focuses multivariate time series form xt=zt+∑h=1qAhzt−h,x_t = z_t + \sum_{h=1}^{q} A_h z_{t-h}, ztz_t white-noise innovation vector (A_h) coefficient matrix lag hh. mainly designed exploratory methodological experiments high-dimensional VMA-type processes. typical workflow : generate VMA coefficient matrices; simulate multivariate VMA observations; compute lagged cross-covariance matrices; construct VMA order statistics; estimate VMA order; check stationarity visualize statistic curves.

title: Tools for Vector Moving Average Order Estimation
text: can install development version VMAorder GitHub : installation, load package :

title: Tools for Vector Moving Average Order Estimation
text: following example simulates VMA(2) process 5 variables 100 observations. simulated data stored fit$x, coefficient matrices stored fit$coef fit$coeff.

title: Tools for Vector Moving Average Order Estimation
text: function covVMA() estimates lagged cross-covariance matrix given lag.

title: Tools for Vector Moving Average Order Estimation
text: function statVMA() computes statistic values set candidate lags. statistics used orderVMA() estimate model order.

title: Tools for Vector Moving Average Order Estimation
text: function orderVMA() estimates VMA order using stable-interval rule. selected order taken one lag statistic enters stable interval. draw = TRUE, statistic curve plotted estimated order marked vertical dashed line.

title: Tools for Vector Moving Average Order Estimation
text: function stationVMA() applies Augmented Dickey-Fuller test variable multivariate time series.
code: station_result <- stationVMA(fit$x) #> Registered S3 method overwritten by 'quantmod': #>   method            from #>   as.zoo.data.frame zoo #> Warning in tseries::adf.test(x[, j]): p-value smaller than printed p-value #> Warning in tseries::adf.test(x[, j]): p-value smaller than printed p-value #> Warning in tseries::adf.test(x[, j]): p-value smaller than printed p-value #> sample size: 100  #> dimension: 5  #> non-stationary: 0  station_result$non_stationary #> [1] 0 station_result$table #>   variable    p_value     result #> 1       X1 0.01208235 stationary #> 2       X2 0.01000000 stationary #> 3       X3 0.01000000 stationary #> 4       X4 0.01000000 stationary #> 5       X5 0.03447247 stationary

title: Tools for Vector Moving Average Order Estimation
text: function plotVMA() can used compare mean statistic curves across different VMA orders simulation settings. example evaluated README repeated simulations may take longer run.
code: settings <- data.frame(   p = c(5, 8),   n = c(80, 100) )  out <- plotVMA(   order = c(2, 3),   settings = settings,   R = 5,   lag = 1:6 )  out$plot

title: Tools for Vector Moving Average Order Estimation
text: Run unit tests : Run full package check : Update documentation : Rebuild README editing README.Rmd :

title: Tools for Vector Moving Average Order Estimation
text: Rows input data matrix treated time points. Columns input data matrix treated variables. package intended simulation studies methodological experiments VMA-type models. functions rely suggested packages tseries ggplot2.

title: Estimate Lagged Cross-Covariance Matrix — covVMA
text: Computes lagged cross-covariance matrix multivariate time series.

title: Estimate Lagged Cross-Covariance Matrix — covVMA
text: x Numeric matrix data frame. Rows time points columns variables. lag Non-negative integer lag.

title: Estimate Lagged Cross-Covariance Matrix — covVMA
text: p p matrix containing estimated lagged cross-covariances.

title: Estimate Lagged Cross-Covariance Matrix — covVMA
text: n p observation matrix \(X\), function computes $$\widehat{\Gamma}(k) =       \frac{1}{n}\sum_{t = 1}^{n-k} X_t X_{t+k}^{\top},$$ \(k\) requested lag.

title: Estimate VMA Order — orderVMA
text: Estimates VMA order using stable-interval rule based statistic curve.

title: Estimate VMA Order — orderVMA
code: orderVMA(   x,   lag = 1:10,   center = FALSE,   draw = TRUE,   tail = 3,   c = 3,   min_width = 0.02,   min_prop_after = 1,   consecutive = 1 )

title: Estimate VMA Order — orderVMA
text: x numeric matrix data frame. lag numeric vector candidate lags. center Logical. TRUE, center column. draw Logical. TRUE, draw statistic curve. tail Number tail lags used estimate interval width. c Multiplier tail standard deviation. min_width Minimum half-width stable interval. min_prop_after Required proportion later lags inside interval. consecutive Required number consecutive lags inside interval.

title: Estimate VMA Order — orderVMA
text: list estimated order, interval result, table.

title: Estimate VMA Order — orderVMA
text: order selected statistics computed statVMA. selected order one lag statistic enters stable interval around 1.

title: Plot VMA Statistic Curves — plotVMA
text: Plots mean statistic curves repeated VMA simulations.

title: Plot VMA Statistic Curves — plotVMA
code: plotVMA(   order = c(2, 5),   settings = NULL,   R = 100,   lag = 1:10,   seed = 123,   nonzero_ratio = 1/60,   min = -0.4,   max = 0.6,   same_lag_matrix = FALSE )

title: Plot VMA Statistic Curves — plotVMA
text: order numeric vector VMA orders. settings data frame columns p, n, optionally label. R Number replications. lag numeric vector lags. seed optional random seed. nonzero_ratio Proportion non-zero entries coefficient matrix. min Lower bound generated non-zero coefficients. max Upper bound generated non-zero coefficients. same_lag_matrix Logical. TRUE, use coefficient matrix lags.

title: Plot VMA Statistic Curves — plotVMA
text: list plot data, order frequencies, plot object.

title: Plot VMA Statistic Curves — plotVMA
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ settings <- data.frame(p = c(5, 8), n = c(80, 100)) out <- plotVMA(order = c(2, 3), settings = settings, R = 5, lag = 1:6) out$plot } # }

what: Simulate a Vector Moving-Average Process — simVMA
title: Simulate a Vector Moving-Average Process — simVMA
text: Simulates multivariate VMA process optional user-specified coefficients.

title: Simulate a Vector Moving-Average Process — simVMA
code: simVMA(   n,   p,   order,   coeff = NULL,   nonzero_ratio = 1/60,   min = -0.4,   max = 0.6,   same_lag_matrix = FALSE,   innov = NULL,   innov_cov = diag(p),   burnin = 0,   seed = NULL )

title: Simulate a Vector Moving-Average Process — simVMA
text: n positive integer sample size. p positive integer dimension. order positive integer VMA order. coeff optional coefficient array dimension c(p, p, order). nonzero_ratio Proportion non-zero entries used coeff = NULL. min Lower bound generated coefficients. max Upper bound generated coefficients. same_lag_matrix Logical. TRUE, use coefficient matrix lags. innov optional innovation matrix. innov_cov Covariance matrix generated Gaussian innovations. burnin Number initial observations discard. seed optional random seed.

title: Simulate a Vector Moving-Average Process — simVMA
text: list containing simulated data, coefficients, innovations, order, dimension, sample size.

title: Simulate a Vector Moving-Average Process — simVMA
text: simulated process $$ x_t = z_t + \sum_{h=1}^q A_h z_{t-h}, $$ z_t white noise A_h coefficient matrix lag h.

title: Simulate a Vector Moving-Average Process — simVMA
Generate VMA Coefficients — simVMAcoef
text: Generates sparse coefficient matrices vector moving-average model.

title: Generate VMA Coefficients — simVMAcoef
code: simVMAcoef(   p,   order,   nonzero_ratio = 1/60,   min = -0.4,   max = 0.6,   same_lag_matrix = FALSE )

title: Generate VMA Coefficients — simVMAcoef
text: p positive integer dimension. order positive integer VMA order. nonzero_ratio Proportion non-zero entries. Must (0, 1]. min Lower bound non-zero coefficients. max Upper bound non-zero coefficients. same_lag_matrix Logical. TRUE, use matrix lags.

title: Generate VMA Coefficients — simVMAcoef
text: numeric array dimension c(p, p, order).

title: Generate VMA Coefficients — simVMAcoef
text: returned array stores coefficient matrices $$x_t = z_t + \sum_{h=1}^q A_h z_{t-h}.$$ slice coef[, , h] corresponds A_h.

title: Compute VMA Statistics — statVMA
text: Computes lag-wise statistics VMA order selection.

title: Compute VMA Statistics — statVMA
text: x numeric matrix data frame. lag numeric vector lags. center Logical. TRUE, center column.

title: Compute VMA Statistics — statVMA
text: data frame lag Sn.

title: Compute VMA Statistics — statVMA
text: lag \(\tau\), statistic $$ S_n(\tau) = F_n(\tau) / F_n(\tau + 1), $$ $$ F_n(\tau) = \operatorname{tr} \left\{\widehat{\Gamma}(\tau)\widehat{\Gamma}(\tau)^\top / p\right\}. $$

title: Test Stationarity — stationVMA
text: Performs Augmented Dickey-Fuller test variable multivariate time series.

title: Test Stationarity — stationVMA
text: x numeric matrix data frame. alpha Significance level test.

title: Test Stationarity — stationVMA
text: list number non-stationary variables result table.

title: Test Stationarity — stationVMA
text: column tested tseries::adf.test. variable labeled stationary p-value smaller alpha.

title: Test Stationarity — stationVMA
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ fit <- simVMA(n = 100, p = 4, order = 2, seed = 123) stationVMA(fit$x) } # }

title: VMAorder 0.0.0.9000
text: Added tools simulating vector moving-average models. Added functions lagged cross-covariance estimation VMA statistic computation. Added VMA order estimation, stationarity checking, statistic curve visualization. Added unit tests, pkgdown site configuration, GitHub Actions workflows.
